coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Title shown most recently as SVP and Director (Wolfe Research). Management repeatedly recognizes him by name and slots him into late/late-middle question slots (e.g. #8/#9/#10), consistent with a senior equity research role who gets recurring access. 2–3 sentence profile.
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Primary coverage lens is cruise / leisure / travel with a consumer tilt (onboard and pre-cruise spend). Questions repeatedly link demand, pricing, and consumer behavior to operational geography (Europe, Caribbean, China).
rhetorical_signature: opens with "Hey, guys. Good morning." in 4 of 5 calls (2022-Q1, 2022-Q2, 2022-Q3, 2023-Q1)
asks concise, investor-oriented follow-ups and often requests an "order of magnitude" or percent; closes frequently with short affirmations like "Makes sense" or "Perfect. Thank you."
tone is direct, collegial, non-confrontational; uses multi-part questions to force quant/qual trade-offs

reasoning_style.justification: He mixes quantitative probes (asks for %s, 'order of magnitude', and referenced '180 basis points') with qualitative competitive/demand framing (land-based gap, CocoCay, airlift, consumer behavior).
reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Multi-part and follow-up oriented: ≈7 of 17 turns contain ≥2 question marks (≈41%), and he often layers two-part questions (e.g. pricing vs share, or timing vs carry-through). He pushes for clarification but rarely confrontational.
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: He asks for explicit guidance and magnitudes (percent, order of magnitude) and used 'basis points' language once (referencing management's '180 basis points' in 2023-Q1). He also asks for percent/bookings and occupancy comparisons across multiple turns.
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: Anchors to the 2019/pre-COVID baseline (cites pre-2019 comparisons in 2022-Q4), to recent management lines delivered in the call (e.g., '180 basis points' in 2023-Q1), and to land-based peers/retail commentary (asks about 'gap versus land-based peers' in 2022-Q1).

core_topics.what_they_press_on: Presses management on the timing and magnitude of European booking recovery, causes of underperformance (airlift, testing, Ukraine), and whether Europe will remain a laggard versus Caribbean into 2023 and beyond.
Asks how China is treated in medium-term assumptions (e.g., 2025 Trifecta targets) and how minimal/important China is to modeling the company's targets.
Requests whether the elevated 'new to cruise' mix seen in 4Q persists into 2023 and asks for an order-of-magnitude sense of that continuation.
Seeks clarity on whether an expense-timing benefit (cited by management as '180 basis points') flows fully into the next quarter's guide or is deferred into later quarters.

recurring_concerns.blind_spots: n_turns < 2 for topics such as deferred maintenance/CapEx (no direct questions from him though Stephen Grambling asked in 2022-Q1) and detailed debt maturity / ECA restructuring (Robin Farley asked about ECA maturities in 2022-Q2; Fred did not). He also asked little to nothing about fuel hedging or loyalty-program metrics — topics other analysts sometimes raise.
recurring_concerns.stance_drift: Early (2022-Q1) he focused on pricing gaps versus land-based peers (2022-Q1). By mid-2022 (2022-Q2/Q3) emphasis shifted heavily to Europe bookings/occupancy and consumer spend; by late-2022/early-2023 (2022-Q4 → 2023-Q1) he moved toward mix (new-to-cruise) and more granular modeling questions (equity investment seasonality and the '180 basis points' expense timing).

queue_behavior.typical_position: #8–#10 most frequently (was #3 on 1 of 17 calls, #4 on 3 of 17, #7 on 1 of 17, #8 on 5 of 17, #9 on 3 of 17, #10 on 5 of 17).
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: When early (e.g., #3 on 2022-Q2) he opens on bookings/Europe: 2022-Q2 opened with a Europe bookings/occupancy question.

adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reframe_deeper: Yes — re-asks or drills deeper on the same topic rather than abandoning it (example 2022-Q2: 'have you seen those European bookings numbers accelerate' following earlier testing discussion; quote: "have you seen those European bookings numbers accelerate").
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.peer_or_industry_meta: Yes — will reference industry/retailer commentary to contrast consumer trends (2022-Q2 asked to 'explain that away' vs Walmart/retail commentary; quote: "sort of explain that away from where you sit?").
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.reactive_followup: Yes — anchors to a specific management phrase/number provided earlier in the call and asks follow-up (2023-Q1 referencing '180 basis points'; quote: "that was sized at 180 basis points").
adaptation_when_favorites_taken.cross_narrative_reconcile: Yes — asks management to reconcile different narratives raised earlier in Q&A (2023-Q1 asks what's driving Caribbean strength vs inline Europe after earlier comments that airlift normalized; quote: "what is driving sort of that divergence?")

cross_analyst_reactivity.explicit_peer_references: no evidence in history; 0 turns explicitly say 'following up on X's question' or name another analyst while prefacing a follow-up. He generally picks up topics raised earlier but does not explicitly cite peers by name.
cross_analyst_reactivity.management_line_anchoring: Count = 2; he explicitly anchors his questions to management lines/numbers at least twice — e.g. quoted management's '4Q new to cruise was above pre-COVID levels' (2022-Q4) and '180 basis points' (2023-Q1).
cross_analyst_reactivity.operator_or_followup_signal: He frequently takes a second (or third) turn when the queue allows: in 4 of 5 calls (2022-Q2, 2022-Q3, 2022-Q4, 2023-Q1) he had multiple back-to-back turns and used them for clarification/deeper drilling (examples: 2022-Q2 multiple follow-ups on Europe and consumer spend; 2023-Q1 follow-up on expense timing).
